channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mass crackdowns see thousands of scam center workers waiting in Myanmar for repatriation
item.description: MAE SOT, Thailand (AP) — A new crackdown on online scam centers has led to over 7,000 people from around the world being held in a Myanmar border town awaiting repatriation, and those helping them say the unprecedented number is straining the resources of Thailand just across the border and leading
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says framework economic deal with US is ready but security guarantees undecided
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A framework economic deal with the United States is ready, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Wednesday, but security guarantees that Kyiv views as vital remain to be decided and a full agreement could hinge on talks in Washington as early as Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A court in Bosnia sentences separatist Bosnian Serb President Milorad Dodik to 1 year in prison
item.description: SARAJEVO, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — A court in Bosnia on Wednesday sentenced Milorad Dodik, the pro-Russia Bosnian Serb president, to one year in prison and banned him from engaging in politics for six years over his separatist actions as tensions mount in the fragile Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: AMER COHADZIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran accelerates production of near weapons-grade uranium, IAEA says, as tensions with US ratchet up
item.description: A report by the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog says Iran has accelerated its production of near weapons-grade uranium as tensions between Tehran and Washington rise after the election of U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of president
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday evening shared a video using artificial intelligence (AI) that showcases his proposals for the potential development of the war-torn Gaza Strip, complete with a golden statue of his likeness and beachfront skyscrapers rising from the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan dispatches navy, air force after China launches live-fire drills with no warning
item.description: Taiwan's defense ministry says China launched live-fire drills barely 40 miles off the coast of Taiwan on Wednesday with no prior warning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to Shiri Bibas and her 2 young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: Tens of thousands of Israelis have lined highways holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us” in an outpouring of grief as the bodies of a mother and her two small sons who were killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip were taken for burial.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis sits upright in an armchair as Argentines in Rome pray for his recovery
item.description: Pope Francis is sitting upright and receiving therapy for double pneumonia while he remains in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield And Silvia Stellacci, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stress and fear roil a tiny, rapidly militarizing Japanese island near Taiwan
item.description: YONAGUNI, Japan (AP) — This tiny island on Japan's western frontier has no chain convenience stores. Nature lovers can dive with hammerhead sharks and watch miniature horses graze on a hill. But the wooded mountain ranges now carry radar sites.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: GABORONE, Botswana (AP) — The Botswana government signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company on Tuesday after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SELLO MOTSETA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin appears to contradict Trump on Ukraine peacekeepers
item.description: The Kremlin appeared to contradict President Trump’s claim that Russia would accept peacekeeping troops in Ukraine. “There is a position on this matter that was expressed by Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov,” Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said, according to Politico.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trapped in Gaza: Residents speak out as Egypt blocks exit, rejects Trump’s relocation plan
item.description: Gazans trapped by closed borders voice frustration, with some supporting Trump’s relocation plan as Egypt blocks exits and Hamas clings to control amid devastation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean hackers behind $1.5 billion crypto hack: Security firm
item.description: North Korean hackers were behind a sophisticated hack that stole about $1.5 billion worth of digital currency from Bybit, a major cryptocurrency exchange, according to security firm TRM.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: American tourist arrested in Paris after allegedly throwing newborn baby out of hotel window: reports
item.description: Paris authorities arrested an American woman on Monday after she allegedly killed her newborn by hurling the child out of a second-floor window, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian treasurer seeking tariff exemption in talks with Bessent
item.description: Australia’s treasurer, Jim Chalmers, will continue to push for steel and aluminum tariff exemptions in his discussions with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent this week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: These 17 countries voted with US against Ukraine UN resolution
item.description: Seventeen countries in the United Nations General Assembly joined the U.S. on Monday in voting against a nonbinding resolution condemning Russia as the aggressor in the ongoing war in Ukraine — more than double the vote against a similar resolution two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation
